content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the current diff plus the affected run/daemon/engine code paths and targeted tests. I found one remaining correctness gap: the new partial-cleanup fail-closed behavior was added only to the single-repo daemon loop, while the multi-repo GitHub daemon path still swallows the same lease-cleanup failure and continues on later cycles.

## Amendments

1. Propagate or persistently quarantine `AppError::LeaseCleanupPartialFailure` in the multi-repo daemon loop as well. The new fail-closed behavior only exists in the single-repo path (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1130-1139`), but `process_task_multi_repo()` still bubbles `release_task_lease()` failures up into `process_cycle_multi_repo()`, where the generic branch at `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:589-610` just logs and breaks the current cycle. On the next poll that repo is eligible again even though the prior task kept contradictory durable lease/worktree state, so GitHub/multi-repo daemon deployments still do not actually block on partial cleanup failures.
   Rationale: This leaves one production daemon mode continuing after a known cleanup-consistency breach. The retained lease makes the failed task visible, but the daemon no longer surfaces the problem as a hard failure and can resume dispatching other work in the same repo on later cycles, which is the exact class of silent continuation this bead is supposed to eliminate.
